mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods show negative or flat 60-day forward returns, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a restrictive but insufficiently high federal funds rate creates negative real yields and forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combination of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of confirmed recession onsets, or the signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signals synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, reducing input cost pressures and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against rolling 3-year windows.
